evidence: RuleOfSigns.lean:50 def signVariations counts coefficient sign changes; module doc names 'Descartes' Rule of Signs' explicitly; roots_countP_pos_le_signVariations (the theorem) already carries Q1379344 but the def itself is untagged
PartialFractions.lean:341, module doc header 'Partial fractions'; theorem is the general existence result f/g = q + sum of r_ij/g_i^(j+1), the defining statement of partial fraction decomposition
Reverse.lean:220 noncomputable def reverse (f : R[X]) : R[X], docstring 'reverse f = f (1/X) * X ^ f.natDegree' -- exactly the reciprocal-polynomial construction (reversed coefficients)
Degree/Defs.lean:60 def Monic (p : R[X]) := leadingCoeff p = 1, docstring 'a polynomial is Monic if its leading coefficient is 1' -- the textbook definition, decl currently untagged
HasseDeriv.lean:59 def hasseDeriv (k : Nat) : R[X] ->l[R] R[X], docstring 'k-th Hasse derivative of a polynomial sum a_i X^i is sum (i choose k) a_i X^(i-k)' matches Q17067052 description directly
UnitTrinomial.lean:43 noncomputable def trinomial := C u * X^k + C v * X^m + C w * X^n, docstring 'Shorthand for a trinomial' -- a three-term polynomial, exact match to Q1670509 description

Laurent.lean:84 abbrev LaurentPolynomial; doc 'sum a_i T^i, i in Z, neg exponents allowed'. Currently only tagged generic Q43260 in decl_to_qid_v2.json -- Q1701125 is the tighter correct QID, absent from universe

Degree/CardPowDegree.lean:44 def cardPowDegree : AbsoluteValue Fq[X] Int; doc title 'Absolute value on polynomials over a finite field' -- return type is literally AbsoluteValue; matches Q4669840, not Q120812 (real-number abs value)
Sequence.lean:45 structure Sequence [Semiring R] where ...; module doc 'sequences of polynomials a_0, a_1, ... such that a_i has degree i' -- literal definition of Q1052437

Derivative.lean:46 def derivative via p.sum (fun n a => C(a*n)*X^(n-1)); docstring 'formal derivative of p' -- algebraic non-limit derivative, tighter than generic calculus-derivative QIDs already on this decl
Div.lean:132 def divByMonic, built via recursive divModByMonicAux (repeated subtract-leading-term loop) -- the standard long-division algorithm; modByMonic (line 137) is the paired remainder decl

Div.lean:137 def modByMonic (p q : R[X]) : R[X] -- the remainder half of the same divModByMonicAux long-division algorithm as divByMonic; paired decl, slightly lower confidence since 'division' concept centers more on the quotient
